title: The Container Door Closed
subtitle: Before the road began, Toyotaki first had to disappear into a 20-foot container.
summary: Before the 306-day road journey began, Toyotaki was loaded into a 20-foot container at Port Klang and shipped toward Kolkata.

title: Traffic Light Equation
subtitle: Two oranges, one laughing driver, one impatient policeman, infinite memories.
summary: At a Kolkata traffic light, the whole world moved too fast except the fruit seller and two glowing oranges.

title: 407 km to Cuttack
subtitle: A winter market, a Tibetan refugee community, and the meaning of having a home.
summary: After driving 407 km from Kolkata to Cuttack, Tuzi met a Tibetan refugee community and heard a sixty-year story of gratitude, resilience, and hope.

title: Twenty-One Minutes in Toyotaki
subtitle: How a small daily practice helped Tuzi stay healthy and alert across 42,049 km.
summary: After attending Inner Engineering, Tuzi carried a 21-minute practice into the small walkway of Toyotaki to maintain health and alertness on the road.

title: From Far Away to See
subtitle: A temple encounter, a sudden blessing, and a sentence Tuzi never expected to hear.
summary: After trusting two men she met in Coimbatore, Tuzi was brought to a Kerala temple and received a sentence that stayed with her on the road.

title: The Green Light of Kasaragod
subtitle: A mosque at night, a warm local community, and the first pause on the rush toward Agra.
summary: On the first day of the rush from Kerala toward Agra, Tuzi stopped near a mosque in Kasaragod and was welcomed by a warm local community.

title: A Bamboo Bed Under the Tree
subtitle: The morning Tuzi woke up and realised someone had quietly guarded Toyotaki through the night.
location: Roadside restaurant, Kerala to Agra route
summary: While rushing from Kerala to Agra, Tuzi slept at a roadside restaurant and woke to find someone had slept in front of Toyotaki to protect her.

title: Thirty Minutes of Moonlight
subtitle: A full moon window, a silent Taj Mahal, and a family photo taken in the dark.
summary: A rare full moon viewing window at Taj Mahal turned into a silent thirty-minute memory under moonlight.

title: The Moving Mountain
subtitle: A huge fabric-wrapped load appeared on the road like a small mountain that had learned how to move.
summary: While driving in India, Tuzi saw a huge wrapped load moving along the road like a small mountain sharing traffic with everyone else.

title: The Green House in Morena
subtitle: A roadside curiosity, a green house, and the most beautiful cow dung cakes Tuzi had ever photographed.
summary: In Morena, Tuzi stopped to ask about roadside cow dung cakes and found beauty in a green house, smiling children, and everyday rural life.

title: The Colourful Day in Gwalior
subtitle: A decorated calf, a tight route toward Nepal, and the hidden pressure of reaching Iran customs by 01/01/2024.
summary: While rushing toward Nepal with a strict route plan toward Iran, Tuzi stopped in Gwalior for a colourful decorated calf.

title: Spreading Smiles at Sugauli Road
subtitle: A roadside stop, a headmaster’s wave, and a schoolyard suddenly full of laughter.
summary: On the way to Nepal, Tuzi stopped to film children playing and was invited by a headmaster into a primary school full of laughter.

title: Chai, Goats, and the Border Gate
subtitle: Small fragments from 02/12/2023: a clay cup of masala chai, goats in clothes, and the customs gate toward Kathmandu.
summary: On the way toward Kathmandu, 02/12/2023 became a day of small fragments: masala chai, the Birgunj customs gate, and goats in clothes after entering through the gate.

title: Serendipitous Bonds and Acts of Kindness
subtitle: When Toyotaki was stuck against a roadside rock, Sanjay Gole and his family appeared with tools, experience, and unexpected kindness.
summary: On the way to Daman, Toyotaki was stuck against a roadside rock until Sanjay Gole and his family helped, creating an unexpected Malaysia-Nepal bond.

title: Hotel Paradise, Daman
subtitle: After the difficult road toward Daman, Tuzi found three beautiful days of mountain air, Himalayan views, and quiet hospitality at Hotel Paradise.
summary: At Hotel Paradise in Daman, Tuzi stayed for three beautiful days after a difficult road, surrounded by Himalayan views and the quiet warmth of a shy Nepalese couple.

title: A KK Mart on the Road to Kathmandu
subtitle: A small Malaysian surprise in Nepal: a familiar KK Mart standing quietly on the road toward Kathmandu.
summary: On the road toward Kathmandu, Tuzi unexpectedly found a KK Mart operated by a Malaysian boss, with an interior that felt just like Malaysia.

title: Goats on the Roof
subtitle: While exploring Kathmandu, Tuzi noticed goats tied on top of a bus — funny, practical, strange, and completely part of the road.
summary: In Kathmandu, Tuzi saw goats tied on top of a bus, a funny and unforgettable glimpse of local road life.

title: Where the Journey Became Stickers
subtitle: In Kathmandu, Abhishek Baniya helped design and apply country stickers to Toyotaki — turning the route into visible memory.
summary: At Aaradhya Sticker House in Kathmandu, Abhishek Baniya, an Indian entrepreneur in Nepal, helped Tuzi turn her crossed countries into stickers on Toyotaki.

title: Lunch Beside the Trishuli
subtitle: A simple lunch stop by the Trishuli River became a conversation, an interview, and a small memory of young local entrepreneurship in Nepal.
summary: Along the Trishuli River, Tuzi stopped for lunch, enjoyed a Thakali-style Khana Set, and met young restaurant owner Sandip Shrestha, turning a meal into a YouTube interview.

title: By the Roadside, a Magar Wedding
subtitle: Drawn in by music and colour, Tuzi stopped by the roadside in Pokhara and was warmly invited into a Magar community wedding.
summary: A roadside stop in Pokhara turned into a warm invitation into a Magar community wedding, with music, food, hospitality, and an interview about Magar traditions.

title: Machapuchare from the Rooftop
subtitle: After navigating a narrow path to overnight in Pokhara, Tuzi was blessed with a clear rooftop view of Machapuchare.
summary: In Pokhara, after a narrow path and an overnight stop, Tuzi saw Machapuchare clearly from the hotel rooftop, with her portrait taken by a local cleaner lady.

title: Beautiful, Dangerous Road
subtitle: Nepal’s road scenery was stunning, but the road condition was frightening enough that Tuzi decided to drive back toward India.
location: Nepal mountain road, returning toward India
summary: Scared by Nepal’s rough and dangerous mountain road conditions, Tuzi decided to drive Toyotaki back toward India, even though the scenery was beautiful.
